content: LiDAR point clouds require pre-filtering to remove ground plane noise before object detection
Stereo camera baseline width directly affects depth estimation accuracy at range
Time-of-flight sensors saturate in high-ambient-light outdoor environments
IMU drift accumulates over time and must be corrected with external reference during long runs
Encoder resolution limits minimum controllable velocity at low speeds
Sensor fusion via Kalman filter reduces state estimation noise by combining IMU and wheel odometry
Silicone-coated grippers conform to irregular surfaces better than rigid fingers
Carbon fibre linkages reduce moving mass but require careful adhesive joint design
Shape memory alloy actuators provide high force density but require thermal cycling time
Brushless DC motors require ESC tuning to avoid resonance at mid-throttle
Hydraulic actuators deliver higher peak force but introduce fluid management complexity
Visual servoing latency above 80ms causes instability in high-speed grasping tasks
Fiducial marker detection provides robust pose estimates in structured environments
Graphene-enhanced battery electrodes increase energy density but complicate recycling
Aerogel thermal insulation keeps battery packs within operating range in cold environments
Redundant power rails prevent total system failure from a single regulator fault
Inverse kinematics solvers must handle joint limit constraints to avoid singularities
Depth cameras struggle with transparent and specular surfaces due to IR absorption
Compliant mechanisms reduce shock loads on gearboxes during hard contact events
